Bash(/tmp/find_unchecked.py << 'EOF'
import re

with open\(r"C:\\Users\\Aslan\\claude_rusty_box\\rusty_box\\src\\cpu\\cpu.rs", 'r'\) as f:
    lines = f.readlines\(\)

# Lines with _m\( calls
target_lines = [2255, 2299, 2309, 2317, 2325, 2335, 2343, 2351, 2361, 2369, 2377, 2385, 2393, 2412, 2549, 2563, 2571, 2581, 3157, 3165, 3187, 3197, 3211, 3219, 3229, 3237, 3259, 3269, 3279, 3290, 3298, 3308, 3316, 3338, 3348, 3358, 3368, 3378, 3388, 3402, 3412, 3422, 3432, 3442, 3452, 3462, 3472, 3482, 3492, 3506, 3516, 3906, 3910, 3970, 3974, 3978, 3982, 3986]

results = []

for line_num in target_lines:
    line_idx = line_num - 1  # 0-based indexing
    
    # Look back up to 15 lines for mod_c0
    has_mod_c0 = False
    start_idx = max\(0, line_idx - 15\)
    
    for i in range\(start_idx, line_idx\):
        if 'mod_c0' in lines[i]:
            has_mod_c0 = True
            break
    
    if not has_mod_c0:
        # Get the handler name and opcode
        line_content = lines[line_idx].strip\(\)
        opcode_line = None
        for i in range\(line_idx - 1, max\(0, line_idx - 5\), -1\):
            if 'Opcode::' in lines[i]:
                opcode_line = lines[i].strip\(\)
                break
        
        results.append\(\(line_num, opcode_line, line_content\)\)

for line_num, opcode, handler in results:
    print\(f"Line {line_num}: {opcode}"\)
    print\(f"  Handler: {handler}"\)
    print\(\)

EOF)
